name: Amarillo Country Club
description: Amarillo Country Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Amarillo, TX. There are 8 concrete courts. 4 are outdoors and 4 are indoors. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, and lights. A membership is required to play. The courts can be reserved.

name: Amarillo Municipal Tennis Center
description: Amarillo Municipal Tennis Center is a place to play pickleball in Amarillo, TX. There are 9 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, lights, locker rooms, a pro shop or equipment store, and trainers or lessons. The courts are free. Reservations are not available.

name: Amarillo Netplex
description: Amarillo Netplex is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Amarillo, TX. There are 10 indoor concrete and hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, trainers or lessons, a pro shop or equipment store, and youth programming. A membership is required to play. The courts can be reserved.
open_play_schedule: Check out amarillonetplex.com for daily open play times!
fees: Members only — Membership price varies based on frequency of play.

name: Family Life Center, First Baptist Church Amarillo
description: Family Life Center, First Baptist Church Amarillo is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Amarillo, TX. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. The courts cannot be reserved. The facility includes amenities like food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, and lights.
fees: Members only — $125 for the year for a family of 4.  Must be a member of First Baptist Church

name: Memorial Park
description: Memorial Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Amarillo, TX. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: Olsen School Park
description: Olsen School Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Amarillo, TX. There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free.

name: Tascosa Golf Club
description:
Non-members for reservations, lessons and events are welcome. There is an additional non-member fee. Reservations may be made on Court Reserve. 

An additional 4 indoor, climate controlled permanent courts will be added in September. 

This venue is a DUPR club, UTR club and home to Paddle Wars teams and offers various leagues for all ages and levels.

name: TGC Athletic Center
description: Come play pickleball at TGC Athletic Center in Amarillo, TX! There are 3 indoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms and trainers or lessons. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: The Cactus Cove Inn and Suites
description: The Cactus Cove Inn and Suites is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Amarillo, TX. There is 1 indoor concrete court. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The court cannot be reserved. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to restrooms.
